Nf3| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | |nd| | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | |nl| | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|= || }} =1.Nf3= ===1...Nf6=== 1...Nf6, keeping the position symmetrical, is the most flexible reply to 1.Nf3. At Grandmaster level this is a very popular move and has been the preferred response of both Anand and Kasparov. From here, the game can go in a number of directions and there are plenty of transpositional possibilities into 1.d4 and 1.c4 openings. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}.
